rulings.Case Title: ARTURO CRUZ VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Schneider Electric Usa, Inc. F/K/A Square D Company¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc. F/K/A Square D Company¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to February 15, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 on the court¿s own motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Contra Costa Electric¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant or that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s facts 14 and 15 state that plaintiff testified that he believed defendant disturbed asbestos-containing insulation in plaintiff¿s presence because he was told by ¿plant insulators¿ that the insulation contained asbestos. Yet, nowhere in defendant¿s statement is there any fact that the ¿plant insulators¿ cannot so testify. Nor does defendant¿s separate statement include any facts showing that plaintiff¿s exposure to this insulation was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Scott Co. Of California¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Mr. Gosney creates a triable issue whether Mr. Ray was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Gosney¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s failure to include any facts in its separate statement showing that Mr. Ray¿s exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Ray¿s asbestos-related illness precludes defendant from satisfying its burden of production on that issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production of showing that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s response to special interrogatory 11 states that, when plaintiff worked at the Shell Oil Refinery in 1966, Dillingham employees removed gaskets in plaintiff¿s presence which exposed plaintiff to respirable asbestos fibers. Plaintiff¿s response to special interrogatory 12 states that plaintiff himself can provide evidence of the facts stated in his response to special interrogatory 11. However, nothing in defendant¿s separate statement concerning plaintiff¿s deposition testimony or otherwise shows that plaintiff is unable to testify that, when plaintiff worked at the Shell Oil Refinery in 1966, he was exposed to respirable asbestos fibers from the removal of gaskets by Dillingham employees. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue that plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing gasket attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to paragraphs 18 and 19 of Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objection number 2 is sustained on hearsay grounds and defendant¿s objection number 3 is sustained on lack of foundation and speculation grounds. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement On Terms That Comply With The Medicare Secondary Payer Act, 42 Usc Section 1395(B)(2)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 9, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion to enforce settlement on terms that comply with the Medicare Secondary Payer Act is taken off calendar in light of the Court¿s understanding that the parties have resolved their differences regarding Medicare release language. If this understanding is incorrect, the parties should send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4pm so stating and then appear at the hearing at 9:30am tomorrow in Department 220. = (220/HEK)
